title: Pass 5.A.2 follow-up: Apple Silicon CI runner for MPS runtime verification
description:
Follow-up to #1100 (Pass 5.A.2 closure): the 6 new `#[cfg(target_os = "macos")]` discriminating tests added to ferrotorch-mps (lib.rs::tests + conformance_mps.rs) compile but do not run on the current Linux WSL2 dev host. Until an Apple Silicon CI runner exists, these tests are reviewable in source only.

Existing CI tests covered:
- mps_device_new_macos_returns_ok_when_available (lib.rs:286-306)
- mps_device_new_macos_rejects_nonzero_ordinal (lib.rs:314-329)
- mps_device_count_macos_matches_metal_availability (lib.rs:344-350)
- mps_device_count_macos_reflects_metal_availability (conformance_mps.rs:508)
- mps_device_new_macos_ok_when_metal_present (conformance_mps.rs:519)
- mps_device_new_macos_rejects_nonzero_ordinal_with_invalid_argument (conformance_mps.rs:536)

Acceptance:
- Add Apple Silicon target to CI matrix (e.g. macos-14 GitHub Actions runner) for ferrotorch-mps
- All 6 tests must execute and either Ok-pass or cascade_skip when no Metal device — never silently skip

Note: this consolidates with #451 (parent MPS kernel-layer issue). May be closed as duplicate if #451 already covers CI runners.
